contextSummary: The richest Form demo set found. form-rhf.tsx wraps Base UI's actual <Form> around a react-hook-form-controlled tree via onSubmit={methods.handleSubmit(submitForm)}, mixing in Autocomplete/Combobox/Select/Slider/NumberField/Radio/Checkbox/Switch/Fieldset. form-zod.tsx uses Form's own errors/onFormSubmit props with zod safeParse. form-native-submit.tsx shows plain FormData extraction.
rankRationale: By far the most valuable Form evidence found, precisely because Form itself is a nearly bare component and the interesting behavior lives in how it's combined with things. form-rhf.tsx is a large real-world 'request a project quote' form that nests Base UI's own <Form> inside react-hook-form's <FormProvider> and overrides onSubmit to `methods.handleSubmit(submitForm)` instead of using Form's native onFormSubmit — direct, working proof that Base UI Form and react-hook-form are NOT mutually exclusive (contrary to what 9ui's and reui's abandoned-Form pattern might suggest). form-zod.tsx shows the alternative path: Form's own `errors`/`onFormSubmit` props with a zod `safeParse`, no RHF at all. form-native-submit.tsx shows the simplest path: native `FormData` extraction. Three complete, contrasting validation strategies from one repo. Feeds: the react-hook-form integration FAQ, the zod-validation demo, and the native-FormData baseline example — likely the majority of the Form doc page's 'Examples' section.

contextSummary: Dify, the highest-starred repo in the corpus using Base UI (148k stars). Re-exports Form verbatim plus its TypeScript types (FormActions, FormValidationMode, FormSubmitEventDetails) individually.
rankRationale: The highest-profile adopter of Form in the corpus (148k stars): even though the wrapper itself is a bare re-export, dify-ui deliberately re-exports Form's TypeScript surface (`FormActions`, `FormValidationMode`, `FormSubmitEventDetails`) as individual named types rather than making consumers reach into the `Form` namespace — real evidence for which typed exports matter enough to a production consumer to warrant their own re-export. Feeds: the TypeScript-usage reference and the real-world-examples credibility anchor.

contextSummary: Selia's basic.tsx demo pairs Form with Fieldset/Field/Input using pure native HTML5 constraint validation: <Input required> plus <FieldError match="valueMissing">This is required</FieldError>, zero JS validation logic.
rankRationale: The cleanest demonstration of Form/Field's built-in browser-native validation story with zero application validation code: `required` on the input plus `<FieldError match="valueMissing">` is enough to show a 'this is required' message on submit, wrapped in a Fieldset with a legend for the group. This is the opposite end of the spectrum from lumi-ui's RHF kitchen-sink form and belongs in the docs specifically to show that Form does not require an external validation library. Feeds: the 'zero-dependency validation' quick-start example and the Fieldset+Form composition pattern.

contextSummary: Cal.com's public design-system registry. A minimal, genuine @base-ui/react/form re-export with a data-slot class merge.
rankRationale: A representative 'minimal genuine wrapper' baseline from a recognizable brand (Cal.com's public design system) — confirms that even design systems investing heavily elsewhere (its Field is comparatively rich) treat Form as pure plumbing needing only a data-slot class merge. Feeds: the real-world-examples credibility list; does not add new technical ground beyond form-002/004/006.

contextSummary: Unlike Kumo's richly-propped Select/Field/Autocomplete components, Form gets only a bare `export * from "@base-ui/react/form"` auto-generated primitive re-export with no visual wrapper at all.
rankRationale: The most telling negative-space evidence in this ranking: the same corporate design system that gives Select and Autocomplete rich, JSDoc'd, Field-integrated wrappers (see the autocomplete/select rankings) auto-generates Form as a bare `export * from "@base-ui/react/form"` primitive re-export with zero visual wrapper — Kumo's own build tooling apparently classifies components as 'wrap with styling' vs 'pass through as primitive,' and Form fell into the latter bucket. Feeds: the 'Form has no visual chrome to theme' framing early in the doc page, contrasted directly against Kumo's Select/Autocomplete entries.

contextSummary: Near byte-for-byte duplicate of coss's and shipfree's Form wrapper (thin data-slot re-export), from an ordinary open-source project-management app rather than a design-system registry.
rankRationale: Included mainly to document the pattern's reach: this exact thin data-slot Form wrapper (character-for-character near-identical) also appears in bytebase/dbhub (frontend/src/components/ui/form.tsx) and revokslab/ShipFree (src/components/ui/form.tsx) — three independent, unrelated production apps converged on the same minimal implementation, which is itself the finding (there is essentially one way to 'wrap' a component with no visual surface). Feeds: the real-world-examples list as a 'this pattern is everywhere' footnote; not independently instructive beyond that.
